seoTitle: New Well vs Newton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Well and Newton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Well vs Newton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Well and Newton.
heading: New Well vs Newton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Well, South Australia and Newton, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Well has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Newton (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Well or Newton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Well has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Newton scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Well scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Newton scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Well or Newton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
